5686	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}
5687	  This is the classical internal-hom base-change isomorphism
5688	  \(f^*\mathcal{H}\!om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(M, \mathcal{O}_X) \cong
5689	  \mathcal{H}\!om_{\mathcal{O}_Y}(f^*M, f^*\mathcal{O}_X)\) of
5690	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-pullback-internal-hom}, specialised to the structure sheaf
5691	  \(\mathcal{G} = \mathcal{O}_X\). For an open immersion the morphism \(f\) is flat and
5692	  restriction \((-)|_f\) is the exact pullback \(f^*\), so the finite-presentation and
5693	  flatness hypotheses of the general ringed-space statement are automatic; this is the
5694	  \emph{favorable} (open-immersion) case of the base-change comparison, in which the map
5695	  is an isomorphism for \emph{every} \(M\), not only the finitely-presented ones.
5696	
5697	  We emphasise at the outset that this is a genuine \emph{natural isomorphism}, not a
5698	  definitional equality, and --- crucially --- that the dual is \emph{not sectionwise}.
5699	  Unlike the tensor product, whose value is computed fibrewise
5700	  (\((M \otimes N)(V) = M(V) \otimes N(V)\)), the dual evaluates to a Hom over an entire
5701	  down-set: for an open \(V \subseteq U\),
5702	  \[
5703	    (\mathtt{dual}\,M)(V)
5704	      \;=\; \mathcal{H}om\bigl(M|_V,\ \mathcal{O}|_V\bigr)\big|_{\mathtt{Over}\,V},
5705	  \]

5707	  \mathrm{Opens}\,Y\), \emph{not} a single fibre \(M(V) \to \mathcal{O}(V)\). Consequently
5708	  the assertion that restriction along the open immersion \(f\) commutes with the dual does
5709	  \emph{not} reduce to a single ground-ring reconciliation. The proof proceeds in two
5710	  stages: a preliminary adjunction-uniqueness rewrite (stage~H1) that re-presents the
5711	  pulled-back dual in pushforward form, followed by the resolution of the resulting
5712	  \emph{residual} into \emph{two} legs, carried out objectwise in the open \(V\) (with
5713	  \(V \subseteq U\)) and assembled over the slice with poset-thin naturality.
5714	
5715	  \emph{Stage H1 --- adjunction-uniqueness rewrite.} The pulled-back dual is initially
5716	  presented in the \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\)-form inherited from the abstract
5717	  definition of the restriction functor, and no sectionwise comparison can begin against
5718	  that opaque form. We first rewrite it into a \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)-form: the
5719	  restriction-along-an-open-immersion pullback and the pushforward along the structure-ring
5720	  map \(\beta\) are both left adjoints to one and the same functor, so by uniqueness of left

5722	  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) --- they are canonically isomorphic, and this isomorphism
5723	  transports the pulled-back dual into pushforward form. After H1 the residual obligation is
5724	  \emph{exactly}
5725	  \[
5726	    (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathtt{obj}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,M.\mathtt{val})
5727	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
5728	    \mathtt{dual}\bigl((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathtt{obj}\,M.\mathtt{val}\bigr),
5729	  \]
5730	  i.e.\ ``\(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) commutes with the dual''. It is this residual ---
5731	  \emph{not} the original Step-4 isomorphism --- that legs (A) and (B) below resolve; legs
5732	  (A) and (B) are the content remaining \emph{after} the H1 rewrite, not a standalone
5733	  two-leg split of the whole step.
5734	
5735	  \emph{Leg (A) --- slice-site Hom base-change (Beck--Chevalley).} The domain presheaf must
5736	  first be transported across the open immersion. Because \(f\) is an open immersion, its
5737	  direct-image functor on open sets is fully faithful with image
5738	  the down-set \(\{W : W \le U\}\); this carries the restricted domain
5739	  \(\mathtt{restr}_V(f_*M)\) over \(\mathtt{Over}\,V \subseteq \mathrm{Opens}\,Y\) to the
5740	  restricted domain \(\mathtt{restr}_{fV}\,M\) over \(\mathtt{Over}\,(fV) \subseteq
5741	  \mathrm{Opens}\,X\), and hence identifies the two Hom-modules computed over these matched
5742	  slices. This is a genuine slice-site Hom base-change --- a Beck--Chevalley square on the
5743	  internal hom across the fully-faithful open-immersion functor, restricted to the down-set
5744	  of \(fV\) --- \emph{not} a fibrewise identity.
5745	
5746	  \emph{Leg (B) --- ground-ring reconciliation.} The codomain of the internal hom is in
5747	  both cases the structure sheaf. Once leg (A) has matched the domains, the unit codomain
5748	  is reconciled along the open-immersion ring isomorphism
5749	  \(\beta_V = (\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}_V \colon
5750	  \mathcal{O}_X(fV) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_Y(V)\): restriction of scalars along the
5751	  ring \emph{isomorphism} \(\beta_V\) is an equivalence, and its ModuleCat-level
5752	  shadow on duals is exactly \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}, carrying the
5753	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(fV)\)-linear dual onto the \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear dual,
5754	  \(R(V)\)-linearly and invertibly. This is the dual analogue, at the module level, of the
5755	  ring-isomorphism tensor equivalence (\cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}).
5756	
5757	  \emph{The leg-(A) atom \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\).} Leg (A) is realised by a single
5758	  \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear equivalence \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,M\,V\), built

5782	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv}\) along the open-immersion ring isomorphism

5786	  % auto-synthesized; it is supplied via \(\mathtt{Module.compHom}\,(\beta.\mathtt{app}\,V)\),
5787	  % restricting scalars along the open-immersion ring map
5788	  % \(\beta_V\colon \mathcal{O}_X(fV)\to\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\).
5789	
5790	  The residual of stage~H1 (``\(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) commutes with the dual'') is
5791	  the sectionwise composite of leg (A) followed by leg (B), packaged over the slice with
5792	  poset-thin naturality; the full Step-4 isomorphism is then the H1 isomorphism followed by
5793	  this composite. The slice-reindexing coherence is \emph{light}, because \(\mathrm{Opens}\)
5794	  is a thin poset and the relevant squares commute by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\),
5795	  whereas H1 and legs (A) and (B) carry the substantive content.
5796	
5797	  It is worth recording \emph{why} the strong-monoidal restriction
5798	  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_adj_substrate}) has no direct dual analogue here. For
5799	  the tensor product the transport \emph{collapsed to leg (B) alone}: because \(\otimes\)
5800	  is sectionwise, pushing a tensor forward reduced, section by section, to a single

5802	  non-sectionwise nature removes that collapse and forces the additional leg (A): the
5803	  domain \(\mathtt{restr}_V(f_*M)\) genuinely lives over a varying slice and must be
5804	  transported before the ground ring can be reconciled.
5805	
5806	  We caution that the naive ``fixed-value-category slice equivalence'' of
5807	  \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv} is \emph{not} applicable to leg (A). That instrument
5808	  operates in a fixed value category over a fixed base ring, whereas the residual of

5810	  that varies with \(V\) and a finer slicing than the sheaf-level equivalence resolves;
5811	  leg (A) must therefore be built directly at the presheaf-of-modules / varying-ring level.
5812	
5813	  Composing legs (A) and (B) gives, for each \(V \subseteq U\), an
5814	  \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear isomorphism between the two values; assembling these into a
5815	  presheaf-of-modules natural isomorphism and sheafifying through the sheaf-level dual of
5816	  \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} resolves the H1 residual. Precomposing with the stage-H1
5817	  rewrite then yields the stated isomorphism
5818	  \((\mathtt{dual}\,M)|_f \cong \mathtt{dual}(M|_f)\).
5819

5821	  isomorphism from the already-established \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} by uniqueness of
